density.instrument.assumptions: Instrument 'flauta' uses externally sourced acoustic metadata via instrumentos/flute.py (GPR interpolation).
Instrument density applies pre-loaded external acoustic metadata to notated pitch and dynamic markings; the pipeline does not analyse audio waveforms.
Quantity scaling model: incoherent source addition (not phase-locked N²).
Qty affects pressure-equivalent density, not pitch-structure metrics.
global_assumptions: Dynamics (p, mf, ff, …) are symbolic score markings; numeric weights are modelling assumptions.
Where instrument modules exist, density uses externally sourced sparse acoustic amplitude tables (GPR interpolation), not live audio analysis.
This analysis uses score/information input only — no audio waveforms were analysed at runtime.
orchestral_mass.assumptions: Dynamic-to-weight mapping is symbolic dynamic weighting for score analysis.
Instrument density component uses externally sourced acoustic metadata where GPR modules exist.
